input: Audit the information hierarchy of this executive report: it has 15 pages, no table of contents, and conclusions buried in the last paragraph of each section.
expected_behavior: Applies Standard 1 (Information Hierarchy), recommends adding TOC for >10 pages, restructures to put conclusions first, applies the Executive Summary > What Changes > Impact > Next Steps pattern.

input: Reduce cognitive load in this technical document that uses 12 acronyms without definitions and presents 15 items in a single bulleted list.
expected_behavior: Applies Standard 2 (Cognitive Load Reduction), defines acronyms on first use, chunks the 15-item list into 3-5 item groups with category headings, applies the 7 plus/minus 2 rule.

input: Review the microcopy in our application: buttons say 'Click Here' and 'Submit', error messages just say 'Error occurred', empty states show 'No data'.
expected_behavior: Applies Standard 4 (Microcopy), rewrites buttons to Verb+Object pattern, adds what-happened + why + how-to-fix to error messages, adds what-is-missing + how-to-fix to empty states.

input: Check the readability score of this executive proposal. Average sentence length is 32 words, passive voice is at 40%, and jargon density is high.
expected_behavior: Applies Standard 5 (Readability Heuristics), flags sentences over 20-word average, identifies passive voice exceeding 15% threshold, recommends Flesch-Kincaid Grade <=12 for executive audience.

input: Run a full UX writing audit on this stakeholder-facing deliverable before presentation tomorrow.
expected_behavior: Applies all 5 standards sequentially, produces UW-01 artifact with hierarchy assessment, cognitive load analysis, scannability review, microcopy audit, and readability metrics with specific fixes.
